expectations.text: Reads and analyzes the PRD document
expectations.evidence: The response states Claude would attempt to read the PRD and fail ('This would fail (file doesn't exist)'), then work from user description instead. No structured PRD analysis is performed -- the response explicitly notes 'No Structured PRD Analysis Phase' as a deficiency. The plan is based on a conversational summary, not document analysis.

The response uses P0 and P1 labels loosely on epics but the self-critique section explicitly states: 'Claude assigned P0/P1 loosely. No P2/P3 items identified. No Out of Scope or Deferred sections.' The Quality Issues section confirms 'No Priority Classification Methodology -- Missing: MoSCoW matrix (P0-P3), per-feature priority rationale, explicit scope boundaries.'

Five epics with stories and tasks are created (Epic 1-5 with stories like 1.1, 1.2 and tasks with hour estimates). However, the self-critique notes task descriptions are 'Title + vague 1-liner' and estimates lack methodology. The hierarchy structure itself does exist.

The Quality Issues section explicitly states: 'No Skill Routing -- Missing: No skill hints assigned to tasks. Skill does: Routes every task to a skill based on keyword matching. Impact: When the coordinator dispatches tasks, it won't know which skill to activate.'

The Quality Issues section explicitly states: 'No Structured PRD Analysis Phase -- Missing: Gap analysis, ambiguity flagging, assumption documentation, stakeholder questions. Impact: Claude proceeds with assumptions it never surfaces. The user doesn't know what Claude guessed vs. what was specified.' No ambiguities are flagged in the plan itself.

The Quality Issues section explicitly states: 'No .claude/ Output Artifacts -- Missing: No PROJECT.md, features.json, kanban.html, or tasks.db. Impact: No shared project context file, no machine-readable feature list, no visual kanban board.' The plan is free-form markdown only.
